sections.paragraph:
Ἐλεάζαρ, ὁ indecl. (Ex 6:23 al.; Philo, Somn. 2, 186.—EpArist; Joseph.: Ἐλεάζαρος, ου [Ant. 4, 152]-αζάρ N25.) Eleazar, in the genealogy of Jesus Mt 1:15; Lk 3:23ff D.—BHHW I 390–91. M-M.

indecl. indecl. = indeclinable

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

BHHW BHHW = Biblisch-historisches Handwörterbuch—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 314
